title: pilosella niphostribes subsp. calodes
intro: Pilosella niphostribes subsp. calodes, also known as the lesser knapweed or Pilosella calodis, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Pilosella niphostribes subsp. calodes can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal herb.
